capabilities.name: Project-scoped instruction file
capabilities.what: A repo file giving the agent standing instructions and context.

anthropic.description: Markdown files you write to give Claude persistent instructions/project context, loaded at the start of every session and surviving compaction.

google.description: Project-scoped rules stored in .agents/rules at the workspace or git root.

openai.description: Repository-scoped AGENTS.md applies project-specific norms on top of global defaults.

capabilities.name: Packaged reusable extensions
capabilities.what: A way to bundle reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.

anthropic.description: Reusable, named prompt bundles packaging multi-step workflows (with supporting files) that both the user and Claude can invoke by name, with support for spawning subagents.

google.description: Namespaced bundles that group skills, rules, MCP servers, and hooks into a single package to extend Antigravity.

openai.description: Plugins bundle skills, app integrations, and MCP servers into reusable workflows for Codex.

capabilities.name: Lifecycle event hooks
capabilities.what: Run custom logic on agent events (e.g. before/after a tool call, on stop).

anthropic.description: User-defined shell commands that execute automatically at specific lifecycle points in Claude Code, providing deterministic control over tool execution.

google.description: Hooks run custom local shell scripts or commands at specific lifecycle points during Antigravity's execution loop, communicating via a JSON stdin/stdout contract.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

capabilities.name: Output & system-prompt customization
capabilities.what: Shape the agent's system prompt, persona, or output style.

anthropic.description: Saved markdown configurations that modify Claude's system prompt and can be reused across sessions.

google.description: User-defined rules in ~/.gemini/GEMINI.md applied across all workspaces.

openai.description: You can define your own custom agents with different model configurations and instructions via TOML files.

capabilities.name: Model selection
capabilities.what: Choose which underlying model the agent runs on.

google.description: Antigravity lets users choose from multiple leading frontier reasoning models to power the agent's core reasoning.

openai.description: Assign specific models to each agent, either auto-selected or pinned.

capabilities.name: External tool/connector protocol (MCP)
capabilities.what: A standard way to connect external tools, data, and services.

anthropic.description: Claude Code connects to external tools and data sources via the open-source MCP standard, supporting servers that run locally or as hosted services.

google.description: Antigravity supports the Model Context Protocol to securely connect the editor to local tools, databases, and external services, both local process and remote host configurations.

openai.description: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the standard way to connect Codex to external tools and context providers.

capabilities.name: MCP server management & registry
capabilities.what: Install, configure, scope, and discover MCP servers.

anthropic.description: A managed configuration file deploys a fixed set of MCP servers with exclusive control, plus allowlists/denylists to filter which servers load.

google.description: Antigravity includes a built-in MCP server manager for integrating external context providers.

openai.description: CLI command to add and manage MCP servers for Codex.

capabilities.name: MCP authentication
capabilities.what: Authenticate to MCP servers (OAuth, tokens).

anthropic.description: Supports env-var, HTTP header, and OAuth 2/2.1 token-based authentication to MCP servers.

google.implementation: OAuth authentication for MCP servers with browser/console authorization flows

openai.description: Authenticates to MCP servers via OAuth using the codex mcp login flow.

capabilities.name: MCP tool governance / allow-listing
capabilities.what: Control which MCP tools/resources the agent may use.

anthropic.description: Granular permission control over which MCP tools an agent may invoke, including wildcard rules.

google.description: Grants agent access to a single named tool on a specific MCP server.

openai.implementation: Manage tools (toggle individual MCP tools on/off and refresh tool definitions)

capabilities.name: Persistent agent memory
capabilities.what: Durable memory the agent keeps across turns/sessions.

anthropic.description: Notes Claude writes itself based on your corrections and preferences, persisted per git repository and auto-loaded into context every session.

google.description: A searchable, cross-session knowledge store that agents can query to recall information beyond the current conversation.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
openai.notes: Reviewed → gap: A code-interpreter resource cap, not durable agent memory across sessions.

capabilities.name: Context compaction (manual + auto)
capabilities.what: Compress conversation/context to extend effective working memory.

anthropic.implementation: Automatic context-window compaction during long sessions

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

openai.description: Manages context window limits in long-running conversations by compacting accumulated history, available server-side or client-side.

capabilities.name: Prompt / context caching
capabilities.what: Cache repeated context to cut latency and cost.

anthropic.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
anthropic.notes: Reviewed → gap: Prompt caching exists at the API/platform level, outside the coding-agent lineup.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
openai.notes: Reviewed → gap: Prompt caching exists at the API/platform level, outside the coding-agent lineup.

capabilities.name: Subagents / delegated agents
capabilities.what: Spawn focused sub-agents to handle parts of a task.

anthropic.description: Specialized AI assistants that run in their own isolated context window, handle a delegated task, and return a summarized result to the main conversation.

google.description: The primary agent uses a multi-threaded async architecture to delegate long-running operations to parallel autonomous Subagents and tracked background tasks, monitored via an interactive panel.

openai.description: Codex spawns specialized agents in parallel and collects their results in one consolidated response.

capabilities.name: Multi-agent teams & messaging
capabilities.what: Coordinate multiple agents that share work and communicate.

anthropic.description: Coordinate multiple independent Claude Code sessions working together as a team, led by a team lead, with a shared task list and peer-to-peer messaging.

google.description: Advanced multi-agent orchestration framework with built-in error recovery, retries, and coordination logic, able to orchestrate agents both synchronously and asynchronously from a central command center.

openai.description: By exposing Codex CLI as an MCP server and orchestrating it with the Agents SDK, you can build multi-agent workflows.

capabilities.name: Workflows / saved automations
capabilities.what: Define repeatable, named multi-step task sequences.

anthropic.description: Persist a generated workflow as a reusable slash command for future invocations.

google.description: Saved markdown files defining a repeatable series of steps to guide the Agent through tasks across sessions.

openai.description: A visual canvas for building multi-step agent workflows that coordinate multiple agents, tools, and control-flow logic.

capabilities.name: Plan-then-execute mode
capabilities.what: Produce a reviewable plan before the agent edits anything.

anthropic.description: Researches and proposes changes without executing or modifying anything.

google.description: Antigravity supports a planning mode that generates multi-turn plans for complex engineering tasks.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

capabilities.name: Goal-directed autonomous iteration
capabilities.what: Set a verifiable "done" condition; the agent loops on its own until met.

anthropic.implementation: Auto mode (autonomous execution with background safety checks, research preview)

google.description: The agent can run local test commands (e.g. unit tests, build scripts) after making code changes and iterate automatically.

openai.description: The SDK self-hosts the agent execution loop, automatically iterating through model calls, tool execution, handoffs, with streaming and deliberate pause/resume support.

capabilities.name: Parallel isolated workspaces
capabilities.what: Work on multiple branches/copies in parallel without collisions.

anthropic.description: An isolation mode that runs agents in separate git worktrees / checkouts so parallel agents don't overwrite each other's files.

google.description: Agents operate in dedicated Git worktrees to isolate parallel work within a project.

openai.description: Each Git-repo automation runs in an isolated worktree to prevent interference.

capabilities.name: Cloud execution environment
capabilities.what: Run the agent in a managed cloud/container environment.

anthropic.description: Runs Claude Code tasks on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure that you can start from the browser or phone and monitor remotely, with each session running in a fresh VM and continuing to run if you disconnect.

google.description: Google Cloud-hosted, fully managed auto-scaling runtime that executes deployed ADK agents in the cloud, managing the agent loop on behalf of the user.

openai.description: Environments let you control what Codex installs and runs during cloud tasks, with caching, secrets, and a default image.

capabilities.name: Background / async execution
capabilities.what: Run tasks in the background or asynchronously without blocking.

anthropic.description: Run a subagent as a non-blocking background task alongside other operations.

google.description: Run tracked, long-running operations asynchronously without blocking the primary agent.

openai.description: Codex runs recurring tasks unattended in the background and reports findings to the inbox.

capabilities.name: Scheduled / triggered runs
capabilities.what: Run the agent on a schedule or trigger without supervision.

anthropic.description: Runs a routine on a recurring cadence or once at a specific future time.

google.description: Users can schedule messages to be sent to their agents while away, with repeatable time-based triggers.

openai.description: Independent scheduled tasks that run on their own without a conversation thread.

capabilities.name: Sandboxed execution
capabilities.what: Run agent actions in an isolated environment to limit blast radius.

anthropic.description: A lightweight isolation package that wraps the entire Claude Code process in Seatbelt/bubblewrap isolation, constraining every tool, hook, and subprocess without containers.

google.description: Provides kernel-level / native OS isolation for terminal commands executed by the Agent in a restricted environment, governed by permission grants.

openai.description: The sandbox is a constrained environment that lets Codex run local commands autonomously without unrestricted machine access, enforced via OS-native mechanisms across macOS, Linux, WSL2, and Windows.

capabilities.name: Tool permission controls
capabilities.what: Approve/deny what the agent is allowed to do.

anthropic.description: A tiered permission system classifies tools by approval requirements and governs whether Claude Code may use a given tool via allow/ask/deny rules evaluated in deny-then-ask-then-allow order, balancing power and safety.

google.description: A unified permission engine secures the environment by representing every sensitive agent operation as a permission resource, evaluated across deny/ask/allow access lists in strict priority order.

openai.description: The approval policy controls when Codex must stop and ask before crossing sandbox boundaries, such as leaving the sandbox or using the network.

capabilities.name: Network / egress control
capabilities.what: Restrict the agent's outbound network access.

anthropic.description: Firewall script restricts outbound network traffic to only explicitly allowed domains.

google.description: Controls network connectivity for sandboxed commands independently of filesystem restrictions.

openai.description: Enables or disables outbound network access, optionally scoped to an allowed domain list.

capabilities.name: Prompt-injection protection
capabilities.what: Defenses against malicious instructions in tool/web content.

anthropic.description: Claude Code includes several safeguards against attempts to override its instructions via malicious text, including context-aware analysis, input sanitization, and isolated contexts.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

openai.description: Built-in guardrail validation that sanitizes inputs, validates or redacts outputs, and checks tool calls to block disallowed requests, redact PII, and detect jailbreak/prompt-injection attempts.

capabilities.name: Security scanning / threat model
capabilities.what: Scan code/agent actions for security issues against a threat model.

anthropic.description: An in-session plugin that makes Claude review its own code changes for vulnerabilities across deterministic and model-backed layers and fix them in the same session.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

openai.description: A security-review plugin and cloud service for Codex that scans repositories for vulnerabilities, validates findings, and provides remediation guidance.

capabilities.name: Dev-container integration
capabilities.what: Run the agent inside a defined dev container / isolated runtime.

anthropic.description: Claude Code can run inside a Docker container managed by VS Code with your project mounted, so commands execute in an isolated environment rather than on the host.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: Stores sidecar metadata; not running the agent inside a defined dev container.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

capabilities.name: Audit logging / telemetry
capabilities.what: A record of agent actions for review/compliance.

anthropic.description: Claude Code exports telemetry data — metrics, events, and traces — via OpenTelemetry to configurable OTLP-compatible backends.

google.description: ADK provides structured, configurable logging of agent behavior using standard library facilities and OpenTelemetry.

openai.description: Built-in tracing emits a structured record of model calls, tool calls, handoffs, guardrails, and custom spans for each run, viewable in a traces dashboard.

capabilities.name: Automated code review
capabilities.what: The agent reviews diffs / PRs for issues before they land.

anthropic.description: An automated PR review service that uses multi-agent analysis to flag logic errors, security vulnerabilities, and regressions, configurable via repository instruction files.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: A human approving the agent's proposed changes — the inverse of the agent reviewing diffs.

openai.description: Codex reviews GitHub pull request diffs and posts a standard GitHub code review focused on serious issues, following repository-specific review guidelines.

capabilities.name: Cost & usage tracking
capabilities.what: Track and cap token spend / usage.

anthropic.description: Claude Code tracks spend and per-session token accounting for cost monitoring of agent runs.

google.description: The CLI lets users monitor their active AI credit quota and consumption directly inside the tool.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

capabilities.name: Evals / testing harness
capabilities.what: Run evaluations / test suites against the agent's behavior.

anthropic.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
anthropic.notes: Reviewed → gap: Permission-evaluation hooks; "evaluation" here is a keyword collision, not an evals harness.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: Runtime agent routing; an "A/B testing" mention is not an evals/testing harness.

openai.description: Configure and run evaluations of model outputs programmatically via API, including evaluation against external models and larger-scale batch evaluation.

capabilities.name: Checkpoint / rewind
capabilities.what: Save points you can roll back to, and undo agent changes.

anthropic.description: Tracks file modifications made through Write, Edit, and NotebookEdit tools during an agent session, allowing files to be restored to earlier states.

google.description: The agent supports rewinding, forking, and non-interactive execution of conversation sessions.

openai.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

capabilities.name: Resume / fork session
capabilities.what: Continue or branch a previous session.

google.description: Users can return to prior conversations to continue an implementation or refine an existing solution.

openai.description: Resume an active Codex session from any signed-in device.

capabilities.name: Remote control (mobile / web)
capabilities.what: Steer or approve agent runs from a phone or remote client.

anthropic.description: Lets you continue a local Claude Code session running on your machine from a phone, tablet, or browser, monitoring and steering it remotely.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.

openai.description: Lets you use Codex from another device or machine to control an agent remotely, including starting/continuing threads, steering work, approving actions, reviewing outputs, and notifications.

capabilities.name: Notifications
capabilities.what: Push notifications on run progress / completion.

anthropic.description: Sends push alerts to mobile devices for remote session events and updates.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: Permission prompts, not push notifications on run progress/completion.

openai.description: Receive alerts when Codex finishes a task or requires user input.

capabilities.name: Chat-surface integration
capabilities.what: Drive the agent from chat/issue tools (Slack, Linear, channels).

anthropic.description: Lets users delegate coding tasks to Claude Code from a Slack workspace by mentioning @Claude, with async execution, context gathering, and message actions.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: Agent-to-agent messaging, not driving the agent from Slack/Linear/issue tools.

openai.description: A connector that lets you delegate work to Codex from Linear issues by assigning or mentioning @Codex, and connect to the Linear MCP server for local tasks.

capabilities.name: Access surfaces
capabilities.what: Where the agent can be used: terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile.

anthropic.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
anthropic.notes: Reviewed → gap: A session-transfer mechanism, not a clean enumeration of access surfaces.

google.description: Not yet covered in the grounded catalog.
google.notes: Reviewed → gap: Sandboxing/isolation, not where the agent can be used.

openai.description: Connect to Codex from the ChatGPT mobile app by scanning a QR code.
